bursaries.name: NSFAS
bursaries.requirements: SA citizen, household income < R350k per annum, registered at public uni/TVET
bursaries.notes: Applications open September 2025. Does NOT fund private colleges.

bursaries.name: Funza Lushaka Bursary
bursaries.requirements: Study BEd or PGCE, commit to teach in public school

private_institutions.name: Varsity College
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Offers own bursaries and payment plans.

private_institutions.name: Monash South Africa
private_institutions.accreditation: IIE, CHE accredited. Australian campus link.
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Merit scholarships available.

private_institutions.name: Boston City Campus
private_institutions.residence_cost: Varies by campus - no official res
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Focus on diplomas and certificates. Degree partnerships with UNISA.

private_institutions.name: Damelin
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Focus on diplomas, higher certs, skills programmes.

private_institutions.name: Rosebank College
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. IIE qualifications. Payment plans available.

private_institutions.name: AFDA
private_institutions.notes: Portfolio required for creative degrees. NSFAS not applicable.

private_institutions.name: Pearson Institute of Higher Education
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Formerly CTI and MGI.

private_institutions.name: Stadio Higher Education
private_institutions.notes: NSFAS not applicable. Strong in distance learning. Merged AFDA, LISOF, Prestige Academy.

tvet_colleges.name: Boland TVET College
tvet_colleges.requirements: Grade 9 for N1, Grade 12 for N4

faqs.a: Admission Point Score. Sum of your top 6 subjects excluding Life Orientation. Level 7=7pts, 6=6pts, 5=5pts, 4=4pts, 3=3pts, 2=2pts, 1=1pt. Max 42.

faqs.q: When do university applications open for 2026?
faqs.a: Most open 1 March - 1 April 2025. Close 30 June - 30 September 2025. UCT/SU close 31 July, UP closes 30 June.
Most TVET colleges close 30 November 2025 for January 2026 intake.

faqs.q: What is the difference between university and TVET?
faqs.a: Universities offer degrees NQF 7-10. TVET colleges offer N1-N6 certificates, NCV, and diplomas NQF 2-6. TVETs focus on skills for jobs.

faqs.q: What subjects do I need for Engineering?
faqs.a: Mathematics and Physical Sciences Level 5-6 minimum for university. Level 4 for TVET N4. English Level 4-5. Check specific uni APS.

faqs.q: Can I study with NSFAS at a TVET?
faqs.a: Yes. NSFAS funds public universities and all 50 TVET colleges if household income < R350,000.
No. NSFAS only funds 26 public universities and 50 public TVET colleges. Private colleges like Varsity, Damelin, Boston are not NSFAS funded.
Council on Higher Education. All legitimate SA degrees must be CHE accredited. Check CHE website to verify private colleges.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a degree and diploma?
faqs.a: Degree: NQF 7-8, 3-4 years, university. Diploma: NQF 6, 3 years, university or TVET. Higher Certificate: NQF 5, 1 year.

faqs.q: Can I apply with Grade 11 results?
faqs.a: Yes, provisional acceptance. You must pass Grade 12 and meet final requirements. Final offer depends on matric results.
National Certificate Vocational. Offered at TVET colleges. Alternative to matric. NQF 2-4. Takes 3 years. Focus on practical skills.
National N-Diploma courses at TVET. N1-N3 = 1 year each after Grade 9. N4-N6 = 1 year each after Grade 12. Need 18 months work experience for full diploma.
Vaal University of Technology. Email: info@vut.ac.za. Phone: 016 950 9000. Applications close: 30 September 2025.
Online at www.nsfas.org.za. Need: ID, parents' proof of income, academic record. Opens September 2025, closes 31 January 2026.

faqs.q: What if I don't meet APS?
faqs.a: Consider: 1) Extended degree programmes 2) TVET college N4 3) Private colleges with lower APS 4) Bridging courses 5) Rewrite matric subjects.
